perturbation.original_text: Party B agrees to pay Party A the Intellectual Property License fees (the "License Fees") and the specified amount of the license fees and the form of payment are set forth in Appendix 2. Party A has the right to exempt the obligation of payment or adjust the amount of the License Fees set forth in Appendix 2 according to the actual circumstances from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B agrees to pay Party A the Intellectual Property License fees (the "License Fees") and the specified amount of the license fees and the form of payment are set forth in Appendix 2. Party A has the right to increase the amount of the License Fees set forth in Appendix 2 according to Party A's discretion at any time.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conflict regarding Party A's ability to change the license fees. The original text allows Party A to 'exempt the obligation of payment or adjust the amount'. The modified text grants Party A the right to 'increase the amount of the License Fees'. This creates an in-text contradiction about the extent of Party A's control over the License Fees, as the original text suggests a potential reduction or waiver, while the modified text only allows for an increase. This could lead to disputes about the intended scope of Party A's discretionary power and what it means.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party B should pay for All Intellectual Property RMB 1,000,000 per year to Party A as license fee. Party B shall pay the license fee to the account designated by Party A before December 31 each year. Party A has the right to determine whether or not to exempt the Party B's obligation to pay license fee at its discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows Party A to 'exempt the obligation of payment or adjust the amount' of the license fees, suggesting a potential reduction or waiver. The changed text grants Party A the right to 'increase the amount of the License Fees'. The contradicted text states "Party A has the right to determine whether or not to exempt the Party B's obligation to pay license fee at its discretion." While both the original and contradicted text allow for exemption, the changed text only allows for increases which creates an in-text contradiction regarding the extent of Party A's discretionary power. The modified text only allows for increases. This contradiction concerns Party A's power to adjust or waive fees, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty, as it is not clear which of the provisions would be enforced and creates an inconsistently of rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is five (5) years unless the early termination in accordance with this Agreement. However, Party A and Party B shall review this Agreement every six (6) months to determine whether any amendment or supplement to the Agreement is necessary by considering the circumstances after the executing of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is five (5) years unless the early termination in accordance with this Agreement. However, Party A shall review this Agreement at its sole discretion to determine whether any amendment or supplement to the Agreement is necessary by considering the circumstances after the executing of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. Originally, both Party A and Party B review the agreement every six months. The change now gives only Party A the right to review the agreement at its sole discretion, meaning there is no fixed period for the revision. This introduces conflict, as Party B no longer has an explicitly defined process to request amendment or revisions which affect both parties. The original term suggests a mutually agreed upon review process to amend, but the modification shifts the reviewing power solely on Party A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any amendment and supplement of this Agreement shall be made by both parties in writing. The amendment and supplement duly executed by both parties shall be deemed as a part of this Agreement and shall have the same legal effect as this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that both Party A and Party B would review the agreement every six months. The changed text states that Party A alone will review the agreement at its sole discretion. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which requires any amendments to be made by both parties. The change effectively removes Party B's explicit right to initiate a review, creating a conflict because the contradicted text dictates both parties must agree on amendments, implying both parties have some mechanism to initiate the review process. Therefore, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, Party A may terminate this Agreement at any time with a written notice to Party B 30 days before such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, Party A may terminate this Agreement at any time with a written notice to Party B 5 days before such termination.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a contradiction in termination notice period. Originally, Party A must give Party B 30 days' notice before termination. This has been changed to just 5 days notice. This creates uncertainty and conflicts, as Party B might rely on the original 30-day notice for business decisions and planning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Early Termination Without prejudice to any legal or other rights or remedies of the party who asks for termination of this Agreement, any Party has the right to terminate this Agreement immediately with written notice to the other party in the event the other party materially breaches this Agreement including but not limited to the obligation under Article 3 of this Agreement and fails to cure its breach within thirty (30) days from the date it receives written notice of its breach from the non-breaching party. During the term of this Agreement, Party A may terminate this Agreement at any time with a written notice to Party B 5 days before such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period for termination by Party A from 30 days to 5 days. This creates a direct inconsistency with the contradicted text, where the previous notice period for Party A to terminate was 30 days, as indicated in the original text. Now, the contradicted text contains conflicting information regarding how many days of notice need to be given, which creates uncertainty and a clear in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.
